summary: Solid coverage of core features (manifest parsing, license status logic, report formatting, config loading) with good Pester organization and mocking. However, several gaps reduce overall confidence: (1) MockDataPath parameter is implemented but never tested—only scriptblock-based LicenseLookup is exercised; (2) Act integration harness (Run-ActTests.ps1) is provided separately and not invoked from Pester tests, leaving the primary integration pathway unverified in the test suite; (3) Edge case coverage is limited (no empty manifests, special characters in names/licenses, version string edge cases like prerelease, or requirements.txt environment markers); (4) Workflow validation uses string matching instead of proper YAML parsing; (5) `.actrc` configuration file referenced in Run-ActTests.ps1 but not shown. The test suite would catch regressions in core parsing and status logic but would miss MockDataPath issues, edge cases, and Act pipeline failures. Tests assume fixture files exist but don't verify them.
